violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the front of the dining room, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026
The floor in the 3 bay sink area are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026
Employee did not wash hands before changing gloves after handling raw chicken. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026
The hand-washing sink in the men's toilet room was lacking proper signage. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026

Employee observed washing ice cream blender part in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Per interview of staff, ice cream blender is only washed, rinsed, and sanitized at the end of the day. The surfaces of equipment that contacts time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as meats and cheeses must be washed, rinsed, and sanitized at least every four hours. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (vegetable chopper and vegetable slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Items were taken to the 3 bay sink to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Cheese sauce, which was previously cooked and cooled, was only reheated to 113 degrees Fahrenheit for hot holding. Chili was observed reheating too slowly, only reaching 72 degrees after over 1.5 hours. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food that is cooked, cooled, and reheated for hot holding must be reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit or above for 15 seconds. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Raw chicken was observed held at 46 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Per PIC, frozen chicken is thawed overnight in the food prep sink, then placed into the walk in cooler. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

The door to the outside, located in the front of the dining area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026
Utensils (ice cream scoop) observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026
Vegetable chopper and vegetable slicer were not air dried before storing. After cleaning and sanitizing, equipment and utensils must be air dried. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Cardboard is used to line surfaces in the kitchen, and in the walk in freezer. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonia sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink, as they expired August 15, 2024. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026
Dumpster area has an accumulation of trash.Dumpster area must be maintained clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026
Floor tiles in the 3 bay sink area are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026

The door to the outside, located in the front of the dining room, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2025
Food observed stored on the floor in the walk in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2025
Scoop observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2025
The area around the dumpster has an accumulation of leaf litter and cardboard. The area must be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2025

The area outside the back hallway door has an accumulation of trash and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2025

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the food prep area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by full mop bucket. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2023
Scouring pad for pots observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2023

The door to the outside, located in the front of the dining room, and back hallway, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/15/2023
The vegetable chopper was not in good repair, broken with teeth missing. New Violation. Correct By: 09/15/2023
The dumpster area has an accumulation of trash and debris. The dumpster area must be maintained clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2023
No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (vegetable chopper blades) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Items were taken to the 3 bay sink to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the sanitizer buckets was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be ( ) ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023

Chili was observed held at 98 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Chili was taken to be heated to minimum 165 before being placed back in hot hold unit. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023
Working containers (spray bottles) used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023

The doors to the outside, located in the front of the dining area, and back hallway, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings on the floor & on shelves. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Terminex PCS. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023
Food observed stored on the floor in the walk in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice bin) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023
Ice buckets are not stored inverted. Water observed inside both of them. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of shelves have an accumulation of dust, soil residue, rodent droppings, and other debris. The oil recycling bin has an accumulation of grease and oil. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023
The dumpster area and sidewalk leading to it have an accumulation of food debris, trash, wood, and debris. Dumpster areas must be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023

The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris, along with rodent droppings. The area near the ice machine has an accumulation of water. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023
